title: Flu and COVID-19 surveillance reports bulletin 2025
body: "  For updates from previous years, see Flu and COVID-19 surveillance reports published up to week 52 2024   Latest  Thursday 17 April 2025  In week 15:   influenza activity decreased across most indicators and was at baseline levels. COVID-19 activity showed a mixed picture across indicators and was circulating at baseline levels Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) activity decreased across indicators and was circulating at baseline levels   For more information, see the flu, COVID-19 and RSV surveillance report.  Flu surveillance data for week 15   flu activity decreased across most indicators and was at baseline levels flu positivity decreased with a weekly mean positivity rate of 5.3%, compared to 6.2% in the previous week; this is based on a percentage of people who test positive among those with symptoms tested overall, flu hospitalisations increased to 1.57 per 100,000 population, compared with 1.40 per 100,000 in the previous week in week 15, the weekly influenza-like illnesses (ILI) General Practice (GP) consultation rate decreased to 4 per 100,000 compared with 4.8 per 100,000 in the previous week   COVID-19 surveillance data for week 15   COVID-19 activity showed a mixed picture across indicators and was circulating at baseline levels COVID-19 positivity in hospital settings remained stable with a weekly mean positivity rate of 4.9% compared with 4.7% in the previous week COVID-19 hospitalisations decreased slightly to 1.33 per 100,000 compared to 1.47 per 100,000 in the previous week COVID-19 ICU admissions remained stable at 0.02 per 100,000 compared with 0.05 per 100,000 in the previous week there were 11 COVID-19 acute respiratory incidents reported in week 15 in total, with 9 incidents in care homes and 2 incidents in hospitals those aged 85 years and over had the highest hospital admission rate, which remained stable at 14.02 per 100,000 compared with 14.46 per 100,000 in the previous week   Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) surveillance data for week 15   Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) activity decreased across indicators and was circulating at baseline levels emergency department attendances for acute bronchiolitis decreased  RSV positivity decreased to 0.7% compared with 1.2% in the previous week   Dr Jamie Lopez Bernal, Consultant Epidemiologist at UKHSA, said:   The national NHS booking system is open for those eligible to book their COVID-19 spring boosters and we encourage everyone who needs this booster to come forward and top up their immunity for the coming months.  If you have symptoms such as a high temperature, cough and feeling tired and achy, try to limit your contact with others, especially those who are vulnerable. If you have symptoms and need to leave the house, our advice remains that you should consider wearing a face covering. Washing hands regularly and using and disposing tissues in bins can reduce the spread of respiratory illnesses, as can ensuring that indoor areas are well ventilated.
